Items.Title: 两个以上的边距可以折叠 | More than two margins can collapse
Items.Description:
顶级编码器

发表于保证金崩溃不仅限于两个保证金！ 在此示例中，4 个单独的边距占用相同的空间：

很难看出发生了什么，但这本质上是前面规则的组合：

  • 兄弟姐妹可以组合相邻的边距（如果第一个元素有 margin-bottom，而第二个元素有 margin-top）
  • 父母和孩子可以组合相同方向的边距每个兄弟姐妹都有一个贡献相同方向边距的孩子。

在这里，在代码中。 使用 devtools 单独查看每个边距：

我们的<header>和<section>之间的空间有 4 个独立的边距竞争占据该空间！

最终，段落的累积边距最大，所以它获胜，并且 40px 将header和section分开。

Juri Strumpflohner - Juri Strumpflohner - May 11 5月11日



原文:

TopCoder

Posted on May 13

Margin collapse isn't limited to just two margins! In this example, 4 separate margins occupy the same space:

It's hard to see what's going on, but this is essentially a combination of the previous rules:

  • Siblings can combine adjacent margins (if the first element has margin-bottom, and the second one has margin-top)
  • A parent and child can combine margins in the same direction Each sibling has a child that contributes a same-direction margin.

Here it is, in code. Use the devtools to view each margin in isolation:

The space between our <header> and <section> has 4 separate margins competing to occupy that space!

The header wants space below itself
The h1 in the header has bottom margin, which collapses with its parent
The section below the header wants space above itself
The p in the section has top margin, which collapses with its parent
Ultimately, the paragraph has the largest cumulative margin, so it wins, and 40px separates the header and section.

Juri Strumpflohner - May 11
Items.Author: topcode007

Items.Title: 边距可以朝同一个方向塌陷 | Margins can collapse in the same direction
Items.Description:
到目前为止，我们看到的所有示例都涉及相邻的相对边距：一个元素的底部与下一个元素的顶部重叠。

令人惊讶的是，即使在相同的方向上，边距也会崩溃。

这是代码中的样子：

 <style> .parent { margin-top: 72px; } .child { margin-top: 24px; } </style> <div class="parent"> <p class="child">Paragraph One</p> </div>

您可以将其视为先前规则的扩展。 子边距被“吸收”到父边距中。 两者正在结合，并且遵循我们迄今为止看到的相同的保证金崩溃规则（例如，最大的一方获胜）。

这可能会带来很大的惊喜。 例如，看看这个常见的挫败感：

在这种情况下，您可能希望两个部分相互接触，并在每个容器内应用边距：

这似乎是一个合理的假设，因为<section>根本没有边距！ 目的似乎是增加每个盒子顶部的空间，给段落一点喘息的空间。

问题是0px 边距仍然是可折叠的边距。 每个部分都有 0px 的上边距，它与段落的 32px 上边距相结合。 由于 32px 是两者中的较大者，因此它获胜。



原文:

So far, all the examples we've seen involve adjacent opposite margins: the bottom of one element overlaps with the top of the next element.

Surprisingly, margins can collapse even in the same direction.

Here's what this looks like in code:

<style>
  .parent {
    margin-top: 72px;
  }
  .child {
    margin-top: 24px;
  }
</style>
<div class="parent">
  <p class="child">Paragraph One</p>
</div>

You can think of this as an extension of the previous rule. The child margin is getting “absorbed” into the parent margin. The two are combining, and are subject to the same rules of margin-collapse we've seen so far (eg. the biggest one wins).

This can lead to big surprises. For example, check out this common frustration:

In this scenario, you might expect the two sections to be touching, with the margin applied inside each container:

This seems like a reasonable assumption, since the <section>s have no margin at all! The intention seems to be to increase the space within the top of each box, to give the paragraphs a bit of breathing room.

The trouble is that 0px margin is still a collapsible margin. Each section has 0px top margin, and it gets combined with the 32px top margin on the paragraph. Since 32px is the larger of the two, it wins.
Items.Author: topcode007

Items.Title: MySQL 万岁：好的旧 MySQL 应该重新焕发活力 | Long Live MySQL: Good Old MySQL Should Be Rejuvenated
Items.Description:
作者： Ed Huang （PingCAP 联合创始人兼 CTO）

这篇文章是我们系列的第一部分。 您可以在这里看到第二部分：* MySQL 万岁：向生态系统创新者致敬***。

想象一下，您是一名应用程序开发人员，您的经理告诉您在一周内制作一个新应用程序的原型。 你会为你的数据库选择什么？ 可能是MySQL。

许多工程师喜欢 MySQL。 但是，其他一些数据库（例如 SQLite 和 IBM Db2）的受欢迎程度正在下降。

根据 DB-Engines 的数据库流行度排名

在Stack Overflow 2021 年开发者调查的 73,317 份回复中，MySQL 被评为最受欢迎的数据库。

我使用最常用数据库的关键字在Indeed上搜索了职位，“mysql”返回了 31,245 个职位。 需要“mysql”技能的职位空缺在顶级数据库中排名第二。

事实上，在 PingCAP，MySQL 是我们的许多工程师在加入 PingCAP 之前就知道如何使用的少数数据库之一。 我就是其中之一。

我使用 MySQL 的 20 年

我第一次使用 MySQL 是在 2003 年我还是一名高中生的时候。当时我想用 PHP 为编程竞赛爱好者开发一个 Web 应用程序（Online Judge），并帮助他们运行他们的代码并检查它是否正确。 为了存储用户提交的数据，我需要一个数据库。 我可以使用 MySQL 客户端编写 SQL 并直接在 PHP 中操作数据库，然后 Apache 呈现动态页面结果。

我是为数不多的基础设施工程师之一。 公司业务增长如此之快，其数据量很快飙升至超过 MySQL 的单机容量。 于是我开始寻求解决方案。 例如，我将 MySQL binlog 用于高可用的主层和辅助层，并应用了水平分片。 当我离开那家公司时，它的数据库集群有 30 多个分片，可以容纳数十 TB。 对我来说，解决这些问题是一次非常充实的经历。

由于这些 MySQL 相关的经历，我有机会结识了许多优秀的工程师，他们在 MySQL 方面有着相似的回忆。 为什么这么多人喜欢 MySQL

它受欢迎的秘诀在于 MySQL 简单易用。 许多用户友好的特性，如 Binlog 也很早就被引入 MySQL。 因此，MySQL 用户的数量在接下来的三年中急剧增长。 此外，许多用户对 MySQL 的使用提出了反馈或直接为 MySQL 做出了贡献，所有这些都导致了 MySQL 生态系统的更加繁荣。 在鼎盛时期，MySQL 成为许多企业的自然选择，因为：

  • 许多工程师都在使用它，为数据库公司提供了一个庞大的人才库。
  • 它是开源的，易于获取和使用。
  • 您可以在互联网上找到几乎所有 MySQL 问题的答案。
  • 它有一个充满活力的生态系统。 大量的开源项目，例如 WordPress 和 phpMyAdmin，严重依赖 MySQL 并使其更加流行。

事情变了

在过去的 20 年里，世界瞬息万变，我们见证了许多重大的技术变革，例如移动互联网的爆炸式增长和当今的数字化转型。 我们的应用场景也发生了巨大变化。 这些变化是什么？ 让我们从数据的角度来探索它们。

数据量呈指数级增长，使得分布式技术成为几乎所有现代数据库的必需品。 它们提供透明的可扩展性，同时保持一致的使用，以便开发人员可以扩展和扩展而无需担心应用程序更改。 然而，NoSQL 数据库为用户提供的功能比 SQL 数据库更有限。 这在 Web 2.0 早期并不是什么大问题，因为虽然数据量增加了，但商业模式相对简单。 以 Twitter 为例：在其最早的版本中，它仅提供一个不超过 140 个字符的巨型共享笔记服务。

如果企业打算构建复杂的应用程序，尤其是那些需要强一致性和复杂关联分析的应用程序，例如 JOIN 和 GROUP BY，SQL 仍然是他们的最佳选择。 不幸的是，传统的关系型数据库，包括 MySQL 和 PostgreSQL，尽管采用了分布式技术，但在很长一段时间内几乎没有什么创新。 他们为单机开发数据库，​​使分片成为处理分布式需求的唯一主流解决方案。 然而，最近我们看到了一些新的数据库项目，可以称为真正的分布式 SQL 数据库。

起初，数据模型从复杂演变为简单，然后变得更加复杂。 这种演变伴随着移动互联网爆炸等技术的重大变化。 当时 NoSQL 和分片数据库的盛行使数据建模受制于一组有限的功能。 有一段时间，数据应用建模不得不简化为键值对、文档和其他灵活简单的模型。

然而，当低垂的果实被采摘时，真正棘手的问题变得更加棘手。 随着金融服务、电子商务等更多行业开始数字化转型以加速在线业务，数据库的应用场景变得更加复杂。 所以，关系模型加 SQL 几乎总是我们最好的数据库解决方案。 在过去的 10 年中，我们看到了许多使用新的领域特定语言 (DSL) 对数据进行建模的尝试——GraphQL 就是一个例子——但我认为它们都无法撼动关系模型和 SQL 组合的主导地位。 对更强大的一致性和实时事务处理和分析的需求一直在增加。 早期，MySQL 的默认存储引擎是 MyISAM，但最终 InnoDB 取而代之。 我认为主要区别之一是 InnoDB 支持强一致性事务。 数据库层提供的语义越一致，应用层必须承担的复杂性就越低。 在处理故障转移时尤其如此。 如果数据库不支持强一致性，那么灾难恢复时就很难保证数据的完整性。 这在大多数金融场景中是不可接受的。

在过去的十年中，数据库管理技术出现了许多创新。 然而，一个明显的权衡是在线事务处理 (OLTP) 和在线分析处理 (OLAP) 的分离。 虽然这种方法在许多情况下都有效，但引入的架构复杂性是一个难以破解的难题。 在这个令人头疼的技术堆栈上保持数据的一致性也很困难。 这给许多需要分钟甚至毫秒级别的实时洞察力的企业带来了巨大的挑战。 由于技术壁垒，公司可能不得不压制他们的业务需求并转向更传统但更容易驯服的解决方案。 近年来，随着数据量和实时分析需求的增加，OLTP和OLAP正在融合的轨道上。

数据的生成和消费方式正在变得多方面。 过去，写入数据库很简单：客户端通过 SQL 语句直接连接到数据库。 如果你仔细看看今天数据是如何进入数据库的，你会发现上游更加多样化； 它可能是来自 ETL 作业的消息队列或批量写入。 批量写入的挑战在于，用户通常更关心吞吐量性能，而不是是否满足 ACID 隔离要求。 在这种情况下，传统的事务机制（例如锁定）将是不必要的。 另一方面，实时性要求的急剧上升导致需要将数据变化同步到下游消息队列以供消费。 变更数据捕获 (CDC) 已成为现代数据库的主流实现。 比如 MySQL 有 Binlog，Oracle 有 GoldenGate，PG 有 pg 复制。

对数据质量的要求越来越高，可观察性的范围从执行面板扩展到数据面板。 随着数据量的急剧增加和分布式数据库的出现，我们对基本的系统信息并不满意； 我们想观察数据是如何被访问的，并从中获得业务洞察力。 以 TiDB 的 KeyVisualizer 为例。 实时反映数据访问的分布情况，让您一目了然地知道哪些数据被频繁访问，访问的特点是什么。

但是，这些可观察性维度仍然仅限于数据库系统本身。 最近，一个新趋势开始出现：数据可观察性，它试图衡量数据的质量。 硬件基础设施正在发生巨大变化。 随着云变得越来越流行，托管服务正在成为主流。 硬件的进步缓解了很多问题。 比如B+Tree解决了传统机械盘读写慢的问题； 但是，这不是现代 NVMe SSD 的问题。 随着现代云环境的广泛采用，云上的共享存储解决方案（例如 AWS EBS）的性能即使不是更好，也比本地磁盘好。 这将深刻改变数据库存储引擎的设计。 另一方面，数据库厂商和云提供商可以在云上提供完全托管的体验，让用户不必担心运维，可以专注于业务发展。 对于在云上提供数据库服务的厂商来说，这也是一个合理的商业模式。

好的旧 MySQL 过时了吗？

目睹了科技界发生的所有这些变化，我不禁想：MySQL已经过时了吗？ 作为一名数据库内核开发人员，我非常担心 Oracle 对 MySQL 的贡献正在减少。

我不知道甲骨文内部发生了什么，他们向社区做出解释会很棒。

即便如此，我仍然喜欢 MySQL 及其生态系统。 但是我们需要更现代的 MySQL 吗？ 绝对没错。 在我的下一篇文章中，我将与您分享现代 MySQL 生态系统中的一些主要参与者。 敬请关注。

这篇文章是我们系列的第一部分。 您可以在此处查看第二部分：* MySQL 万岁：向生态系统创新者致敬***。



原文:

Author: Ed Huang (Co-Founder & CTO at PingCAP)

This post is the first part in our series. You can see the second part **here: *Long Live MySQL: Kudos to the Ecosystem Innovators***.

Imagine you’re an application developer, and your manager tells you to prototype a new application in a week. What would you choose for your database? Probably MySQL.

MySQL is ubiquitous

Many engineers love MySQL. DB-Engines reports MySQL is the second most popular database; however, some other databases, such as SQLite and IBM Db2, are decreasing in popularity.

Database popularity ranking according to DB-Engines

From the 73,317 responses of Stack Overflow’s 2021 Developer Survey, MySQL ranked as the most popular database.

Database popularity ranking according to Stack Overflow’s 2021 Developer Survey

MySQL is also popular when it comes to hiring. I searched for jobs on Indeed with the keywords of top used databases, and there were 31,245 jobs returned for “mysql.” Job openings that require a “mysql” skillset are the second highest among the top databases.

MySQL is ubiquitous. In fact, at PingCAP, MySQL was one of the few databases a lot of our engineers knew how to use before they joined PingCAP. I was one of them.

My 20 years with MySQL

I used MySQL for the first time when I was a high school student in 2003. Back then, I wanted to develop a web application (Online Judge) with PHP for programming competition hobbyists and help them run their code and check whether it was correct. To store the data users submitted, I needed a database. MySQL was my best choice because it was so straightforward and user friendly. I could use MySQL client to write SQL and operate the database in PHP directly and then Apache rendered the dynamic page results.

In 2012, I joined a startup company whose infrastructure was based on MySQL. I was one of the few infrastructure engineers. The company business grew so fast, its data volume soon spiked beyond MySQL’s single-machine capacity. So I began to seek solutions. For example, I used MySQL binlog for highly available primary and secondary layers and applied horizontal sharding. When I left that company, its database cluster had more than 30 shards which could hold dozens of terabytes. For me, solving these issues was a very fulfilling experience.

Because of these MySQL-related experiences, I had the opportunity to meet many excellent engineers who had similar memories working with MySQL. In 2015, when I decided to start my own business and build a brand new database, MySQL was the first ecosystem that popped into my mind.

Why so many people love MySQL

MySQL’s popularity is unquestionable, although its earlier versions were not as advanced as what we see today. The secret sauce behind its popularity is that MySQL is simple and easy to use. Many user-friendly features like Binlog were introduced to MySQL early as well. As a result, the number of MySQL users grew dramatically in the following three decades. In addition, many users gave their feedback on using MySQL or contributed to MySQL directly, all of which led to a more prosperous MySQL ecosystem. In its heyday, MySQL became a natural choice for many businesses because:

  • Many engineers are using it, making a large talent pool available for database companies.
  • It is open-sourced, making it easy to obtain and use.
  • You could find the answers to almost any MySQL question on the internet.
  • It has a vibrant ecosystem. A large number of open source projects, such as WordPress and phpMyAdmin, heavily relied on MySQL and made it even more prevalent.

Things have changed

During the past 20 years, the world has been changing rapidly and we have witnessed many significant changes in technology such as the explosion of the mobile internet and today’s digital transformation. Our application scenarios have changed enormously as well. What are these changes? Let’s explore them from a data perspective.

Data volume increased exponentially, making distributed technology a must for almost all modern databases. NoSQL databases do a very good job of building a distributed architecture. They provide transparent scalability while maintaining consistent usage, so that developers can scale in and out without worrying about application change. However, NoSQL databases provide users with an even more limited set of functions than SQL databases. This was not a big problem in the early days of Web 2.0 because although data volume increased, business models were relatively simple. Take Twitter for example: in its earliest versions, it provided nothing more than a giant shared note service within a limit of 140 characters.

If enterprises plan to build complicated applications, especially those that require strong consistency and complex association analysis such as JOIN and GROUP BY, SQL is still their best option. Unfortunately, traditional relational databases including MySQL and PostgreSQL made few innovations for a long time even though they embraced distributed technology. They developed their databases for a single machine, making sharding the only mainstream solution to deal with distributed requirements. Recently, however, we have seen some new database projects that can be called real distributed SQL databases.

At first, data models evolved from complicated to simple, and then became even more complicated. This evolution comes with the significant change in technology like the mobile internet explosion. The then-prevalence of NoSQL and sharded databases made data modeling subject to a limited set of functions. For a while, data application modeling had to be simplified to key-value pairs, documents, and other flexible and simple models.

However, when the low-hanging fruit is picked, the really hard problems get trickier. As more industries such as financial services and e-commerce begin their digital transformation to accelerate their online business, the databases’ application scenarios are becoming more complicated. So, the relational model plus SQL is almost always our best database solution. In the last 10 years, we have seen many attempts to model data with new domain-specific languages (DSL)—GraphQL is one example—but I think none of them can shake the dominant place of a relational model plus SQL combination. Google described this situation succinctly in its research paper “F1: A Distributed SQL Database That Scales”: 

"When we sought a replacement for Google’s MySQL data store for the AdWords product, that option was simply not feasible: the complexity of dealing with a non-ACID data store in every part of our business logic would be too great, and there was simply no way our business could function without SQL queries."

The demand has been increasing for stronger consistency and real-time transactional processing and analytics. In the early days, MySQL’s default storage engine was MyISAM, but eventually InnoDB took its place. I think one of the main differences is that InnoDB supports strongly consistent transactions. The more consistent semantics the database layer provides, the less complexity the application layer has to bear. This is especially true when you are dealing with failovers. If the database does not support strong consistency, it is difficult to guarantee data integrity when performing a disaster recovery. This is not acceptable in most financial scenarios.

In the last decade, there have been many innovations in database management technologies. However, one obvious trade off is the separation of Online Transactional Processing (OLTP) and Online Analytical Processing (OLAP). Streaming technologies such as Apache Kafka, Apache Storm, and Apache Spark have emerged to form the so-called “lambda architecture,” which combines the two systems and eliminates the latency of extract, transform, load (ETL). While this approach works in many situations, the architectural complexity introduced has been a tough nut to crack. It is also difficult to keep the data consistent on this head-scratching technology stack. This has caused great challenges for many businesses that require real-time insights at the minute or even millisecond level. Due to the technical barriers, companies might have to suppress their business needs and turn to solutions that are more traditional but easier to tame. As data volume and the demand of real-time analytics have risen in recent years, OLTP and OLAP are on the track of integration.

The way data is generated and consumed is becoming multifaceted. In the past, writing to a database was simple: the client connected directly to the DB through SQL statements. If you take a closer look at how data enters the DB today, you can see that the upstream is much more diversified; it could be a message queue or batch writing from an ETL job. The challenge for batch writing is that users normally care more about the throughput performance than whether the ACID isolation requirements are met. In such scenarios, traditional transaction mechanisms such as locking would be unnecessary. On the other hand, the dramatic rise in real-time requirements lead to the need for data change synchronization to a downstream message queue for consumption. Change Data Capture (CDC) has become a mainstream implementation for modern databases. For example, MySQL has Binlog, Oracle has GoldenGate, and PG has pg replication.

Data quality is increasingly required and the scope of observability extends from the execution panel to the data panel. Twenty years ago, most of our focus was on simple database system metrics such as queries per second (QPS), transactions per second (TPS), latency, and query plan level. With the dramatic increase in data volume and the emergence of distributed databases, we are not satisfied with basic system information; we want to observe how data is accessed, and from that, gain business insights. Take TiDB’s KeyVisualizer as an example. It reflects the distribution of data access in real time so that you can tell at a glance which data is frequently accessed and what are the characteristics of the access.

TiDB’s KeyVisualizer

However, these observability dimensions are still limited to the database system itself. Recently, a new trend has started to emerge: data observability, which tries to measure the quality of data. Anomalo is a good example, and I think this will be a very promising direction for database products.

The hardware infrastructure is changing dramatically. As the cloud is becoming more prevalent, managed services are becoming the mainstream. When MySQL was released, there were no SSDs or 20G NICs. The advances in hardware have alleviated many problems. For example, B+Tree solved the problem of the slow reads and writes of traditional mechanical disks; however, this is not an issue with modern NVMe SSDs. With wide adoption of modern cloud environments, shared storage solutions on the cloud, such as AWS EBS, perform as well, if not better, than local disks. This will profoundly change the design of database storage engines. On the other hand, database vendors and cloud providers can offer fully managed experience on the cloud, so that users won’t have to worry about operations and maintenance and can focus on the business development. This is also a reasonable business model for vendors providing database services on the cloud.

Is good old MySQL outdated?

Having witnessed all those changes happening in the technology world, I could not help but think: is MySQL outdated? As a database kernel developer, I am more than concerned that the contribution from Oracle to MySQL is dwindling.

The growth trend of the number of MySQL contributors

MySQL’s commits trend during past 3 years

I don’t know what’s going on inside Oracle, and it would be great for them to make an explanation to the community.

Even so, I still love MySQL and its ecosystem. The MySQL ecosystem will not go away. MySQL applications and developers are still the foundations of the database market. But do we need a more modern MySQL? Absolutely yes. In my next post, I will share with you some major players in the modern MySQL ecosystem. Stay tuned.

This post is the first part in our series. You can see the second part here: *Long Live MySQL: Kudos to the Ecosystem Innovators***.
Items.Author: yuxi903

Items.Title: 使用 Keda 和 Kubernetes 实现事件驱动的 Auto Scaling | Implementing Event Driven Auto Scaling with Keda and Kubernetes
Items.Description:
使用 Keda、Spring 和 RabbitMQ 实现 Kubernetes 事件驱动的 Auto Scaling

科达

使用 KEDA，您可以明确映射您想要使用事件驱动规模的应用程序，而其他应用程序继续运行。 这使得 KEDA 成为与任何数量的其他 Kubernetes 应用程序或框架一起运行的灵活且安全的选择。

自动缩放是 Kubernetes 最喜欢的功能之一。 当我们谈论 Kubernetes 自动缩放功能时，会自动想到 Horizo​​ntal Pod Autoscaler (HPA)。 与容器外的不同组件集成的复杂和分布式应用程序。 这些组件基本上是事件源，可以是 MongoDB、Azure 服务总线、RabbitMQ 队列长度、Kafka 主题滞后、Redis 流等。HPA 无法根据这些组件的指标扩展 pod。 为了缓解这种情况，KEDA 在 Horizo​​ntal Pod Autoscaler 之上提供了扩展功能。 使用 KEDA，您可以明确映射您想要使用事件驱动规模的应用程序，而其他应用程序继续运行。 它还允许您将 Scaler 集成到 Kubernetes 集群中。 它将帮助您根据来自这些事件源的不同指标来扩展您的 pod。
科达的架构

操作员/代理：激活和停用 Kubernetes 部署以在没有事件的情况下从零扩展到零。

  • 指标：充当 Kubernetes 指标服务器，向 Horizo​​ntal Pod Autoscaler 公开丰富的事件数据，如队列长度或流延迟，以推动横向扩展。

    • 运行和实施科达

    执行该项目时使用了以下技术，需要安装一些项目：

    • 码头工人
    • 爪哇 17
    • 马文
    • 兔MQ

    准备环境

    在 Kubernetes 集群中安装 Keda

    使用 minikube 启动我们的集群。 按照以下命令启动 Kubernetes 集群：

    运行该命令后，您还需要另一个命令来在我们的集群中安装 Keda，执行命令：

     kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kedacore/keda/releases/download/v2.0.0/keda-2.0.0.yaml

    您使用此命令来咨询您的 Keda 安装：

    在 Kubernetes 集群中安装 RabbitMQ

    为此，我使用了 RabbitMQ 集群运算符。 按照以下命令在集群中安装 RabbitMQ：

     kubectl apply -f https://github.com/rabbitmq/cluster-operator/releases/latest/download/cluster-operator.yml

    为您检查集群上的 RabbitMQ 部署：

     kubectl get all -n rabbitmq-system

    在项目event-service-sender中，您有 k8s 路径，并且您正在运行配置 Rabbit 的命令：

     kubectl apply -f k8s/rabbitmq-keda-cluster.yaml

    为您验证 Replica 和所有配置到 Rabbit：

     kubectl describe RabbitmqCluster rabbitmq-keda-cluster kubectl get all -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=rabbitmq

    要在 localhost 中完成兔子管理，您需要执行以下命令：

     kubectl port-forward service/rabbitmq-keda-cluster 15672

    现在您可以使用 User:guest 和 Password:guest 访问此端点http://localhost:15672来控制您的 RabbitMQ 并创建您的队列。

    在 Kubernetes 中部署发布者和消费者

    配置 Kubernetes 集群和 Rabbit 后，我​​们的环境就可以运行我们的应用程序并使用 Keda 进行概念验证。

    四个您部署事件发送器和事件接收器，您需要在终端上导航到两个项目的路径并访问您需要执行此命令：

     kubectl apply -f k8s/app-deployment.yaml

    四、您部署需要执行此命令的事件接收器：

     kubectl apply -f k8s/app-deployment.yaml

    您需要为这两种服务提供咨询 pod：

    为在终端的两个不同实例中运行两个命令的两个应用程序启用 localhost：

     kubectl port-forward svc/event-service-receiver 9095 kubectl port-forward svc/event-service-sender 9096

    现在，您可以使用 jmeter 进行压力测试，并遵循消费者服务中的可伸缩性



    原文:

    Implementing Kubernetes Event Driven Auto Scaling with Keda, Spring and RabbitMQ

    Keda

    KEDA is a single-purpose and lightweight component that can be added into any Kubernetes cluster. KEDA works alongside standard Kubernetes components like the Horizontal Pod Autoscaler and can extend functionality without overwriting or duplication. With KEDA you can explicitly map the apps you want to use event-driven scale, with other apps continuing to function. This makes KEDA a flexible and safe option to run alongside any number of other Kubernetes applications or frameworks.

    Autoscaling is one the favorite features of Kubernetes. When we talk about Kubernetes autoscaling features, then the Horizontal Pod Autoscaler (HPA) will automatically come into mind. HPA is responsible for scaling of pods based on CPU or RAM usage.

    Complex and distributed applications which integrate with different components outside of the containers. Those components are basically event sources and can be anything like MongoDB, Azure Service Bus, Length of RabbitMQ queue, Kafka topic lag, Redis Stream, etc. The HPA is not able to scale the pods based on metrics from these components. To mitigate this kind of situation, KEDA comes with extended functionalities on top of the Horizontal Pod Autoscaler. With KEDA you can explicitly map the apps you want to use event-driven scale, with other apps continuing to function. It also allows you to integrate a Scaler into your Kubernetes cluster. It will help you to scale your pods based on the different metrics from those event sources.
    KEDA is a single-purpose and lightweight component that can be added into any Kubernetes cluster. KEDA works alongside standard Kubernetes components like the Horizontal Pod Autoscaler and can extend functionality without overwriting or duplication.

    Architecture of KEDA

    The principal layer in KEDA architecture is:

    • Operator/Agent: Activates and deactivates Kubernetes Deployments to scale to and from zero on no events.
    • Scalers: Connects to an external event source and feeds custom metrics for a specific event source. A current list of scalers is available on the KEDA home page.
    • Metrics: Acts as a Kubernetes metrics server that exposes rich event data like queue length or stream lag to the Horizontal Pod Autoscaler to drive scale out.

    Running and Implementing Keda

    The following technologies were used to carry out the project and it is necessary to install some items:

    • Docker
    • Java 17
    • Maven
    • SpringBoot
    • Kubernetes(Minikube)
    • RabbitMQ

    Preparing Environment

    Install Keda in Kubernetes Cluster

    Keda is a solution for running in kubernetes cluster. Use minikube to start our cluster. Follow the command below to start the kubernetes cluster:

    After running the command, you need another command to install Keda in our cluster, follow the command:

    kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kedacore/keda/releases/download/v2.0.0/keda-2.0.0.yaml

    You used this command for consulting your installation of Keda:

    Installing RabbitMQ in Kubernetes Cluster

    RabbitMQ improves Operator approach. For that I have used the RabbitMQ cluster operator. Follow the command to install RabbitMQ in your cluster:

    kubectl apply -f https://github.com/rabbitmq/cluster-operator/releases/latest/download/cluster-operator.yml

    For you check RabbitMQ deployment on the cluster:

    kubectl get all -n rabbitmq-system

    In the project event-service-sender, you have the k8s path and you are running the command for configure Rabbit:

    kubectl apply -f k8s/rabbitmq-keda-cluster.yaml

    For you to verify Replica and All Configuration to the Rabbit:

    kubectl describe RabbitmqCluster rabbitmq-keda-cluster
kubectl get all -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=rabbitmq

    To finalize the rabbit management in the localhost, you need to execute this command:

    kubectl port-forward service/rabbitmq-keda-cluster 15672

    Now you can access this endpoint http://localhost:15672 using User:guest and Password: guest to control your RabbitMQ and Create your Queue.

    Deployment Publisher and Consumer in Kubernetes

    After you configure Kubernetes cluster and Rabbit, our environment is ready for running our applications and proof of concept with Keda.

    Four you deploy Event Sender and Event Receiver, you need navigate on terminal to the path for both project and access the you need execute this command:

    kubectl apply -f k8s/app-deployment.yaml

    Four you deploy the Event Receiver you need execute this command:

    kubectl apply -f k8s/app-deployment.yaml

    You need consulting pods for both services:

    For enabling localhost to both applications running the two commands in two different instances to the terminal:

    kubectl port-forward svc/event-service-receiver 9095
kubectl port-forward svc/event-service-sender 9096

    Now, you can use jmeter for stress test and you follow the scalability in your consumer service

Items.Title: 更大的利润获胜 | The bigger margin wins
Items.Description:
顶级编码器

发表于当边距不对称时怎么办？ 比如说，顶部元素需要下面 72px 的空间，而底部元素只需要 24px？

较大的数字获胜。

如果您将保证金视为“个人空间”，这会让人感觉很直观。 在历史的这一刻，保持 6 英尺的距离是社会责任。 如果有人想要更多的空间——比如 8 英尺——我们需要保持 8 英尺的距离才能满足两个个人空间的要求。

Joao Carlos Sousa do Vale - Joao Carlos Sousa do Vale - May 5 5月5日



原文:

TopCoder

Posted on May 13

What about when the margins are asymmetrical? Say, the top element wants 72px of space below, while the bottom element only needs 24px?

The bigger number wins.

This one feels intuitive if you think of margin as "personal space". In this moment in history, it's socially responsible to keep 6 feet apart. If someone wants even more space — say, 8 feet — we'll need to keep 8 feet apart in order to satisfy both personal-space requirements.

Joao Carlos Sousa do Vale - May 5
Items.Author: topcode007

Items.Title: 嵌套并不能防止折叠 | Nesting doesn't prevent collapsing
Items.Description:
好吧，这就是它开始变得奇怪的地方。 考虑以下代码：

 <style> p { margin-top: 48px; margin-bottom: 48px; } </style> <div> <p>Paragraph One</p> </div> <p>Paragraph Two</p>

我们将第一段放入包含<div>的内容中，但边距仍会塌陷！

怎么会这样？ 好吧，事实证明，我们中的许多人对利润的运作方式存在误解。

边距是为了增加兄弟姐妹之间的距离。 它_不是_意味着增加孩子与其父母边界框之间的差距； 这就是填充的用途。

边距总是会尝试增加兄弟元素之间的距离，即使这意味着将边距转移到父元素！ 在这种情况下，效果与我们将边距应用于父<div>而不是子<p>的效果相同。

必须满足一些条件才能将保证金转移到父级（并折叠）：

  • 中间没有其他元素（参见前面关于<br>的规则）。
  • 父元素没有设置height 。
  • 父元素沿相关边缘没有任何填充或边框。 最后一种情况很常见，所以让我们看一个简单的例子。 在这种情况下，我们的嵌套子级不能将边距与下一段结合起来，因为父级有一些填充：

您可以将填充/边框视为一种墙； 如果它位于两个边距之间，它们就不会塌陷，因为有一堵墙挡住了路。 宽度也无关紧要； 即使是 1px 的填充也会干扰边距折叠。



原文:

Alright, here's where it starts to get weird. Consider the following code:

<style>
  p {
    margin-top: 48px;
    margin-bottom: 48px;
  }
</style>
<div>
  <p>Paragraph One</p>
</div>
<p>Paragraph Two</p>

We're dropping our first paragraph into a containing <div>, but the margins will still collapse!

How can this be? Well, it turns out that many of us have a misconception about how margins work.

Margin is meant to increase the distance between siblings. It is _not _meant to increase the gap between a child and its parent's bounding box; that's what padding is for.

Margin will always try and increase distance between siblings, even if it means transferring margin to the parent element! In this case, the effect is the same as if we had applied the margin to the parent <div>, not the child <p>.

“But that can't be!”, I can hear you saying. “I've used margin before to increase the distance between the parent and the first child!”

There are some conditions that must be satisfied in order for the margin to be transferred to the parent (and collapsed):

  • No other elements in-between (see earlier rule, about the <br>).
  • The parent element doesn't have a heightset.
  • The parent element doesn't have any padding or border along the relevant edge. That last condition is really common, so let's look at a quick example. In this case, our nested child can't combine margin with the next paragraph, because the parent has some padding in the way:

You can think of padding/border as a sort of wall; if it sits between two margins, they can't collapse, because there's a wall in the way. The width doesn't matter, either; even 1px of padding will interfere with margin collapse.

Items.Title: 只有相邻元素折叠 | Only adjacent elements collapse
Items.Description:
顶级编码器

发表于使用<br />标签（换行符）来增加块元素之间的空间有些常见。

 <style> p { margin-top: 32px; margin-bottom: 32px; } </style> <p>Paragraph One</p> <br /> <p>Paragraph Two</p>

遗憾的是，这对我们的利润率产生了不利影响：

元素需要在 DOM 中相邻才能使它们的边距折叠*。

Mahmoud Harmouch - Mahmoud Harmouch - Apr 24 4月24日

Mohammed Nadeem Shareef - Mohammed Nadeem Shareef - Apr 22 4月22日



原文:

TopCoder

Posted on May 13

It is somewhat common to use the <br /> tag (a line-break) to increase space between block elements.

<style>
  p {
    margin-top: 32px;
    margin-bottom: 32px;
  }
</style>
<p>Paragraph One</p>
<br />
<p>Paragraph Two</p>

Regrettably, this has an adverse effect on our margins:

The <br /> tag is invisible and empty, but any element between two others will block margins from collapsing. Elements need to be adjacent in the DOM for their margins to collapse*.

Mahmoud Harmouch - Apr 24

Mohammed Nadeem Shareef - Apr 22

Items.Title: InfoAbout.me | InfoAbout.me
Items.Description:
这个应用程序的主要目的是让用户使用这个应用程序在各种社交媒体平台上查看自己上传的内容，这就是为什么应用程序被命名为InfoAbout.me的原因。

大多数时候，全球各地的人们必须在一天内访问各种应用程序或社交媒体平台，以检查他们在社交媒体平台上上传的内容，因此牢记这个问题，我制作了这个 Web 应用程序，人们可以在其中查看上传的内容他/她使用这个单一应用程序在 twitter 和 Dev 上发布的内容。

由于时间限制，我只集成了两个应用程序（Twitter + Dev）简单的功能，所以使用InfoAbout.me的用户只能看到他们上传的内容，不能对他们上传的内容进行任何操作。

将来这个应用程序将由我增强，并且像 auth2 等 appwrite 服务也将在其中实现。

目前，此应用程序允许用户使用 InfoAbout.me 登录，如果用户没有帐户，则用户需要创建一个帐户。

成功登录后，用户需要通过 Twitter 和 Dev 授权 InfoAbout.me 应用程序，以便 infoAbout.me 可以成功地从这些应用程序中获取用户数据。

提交类别：

链接到代码

这个项目是使用Redux和Redux Toolkit模板通过Create React App引导的。

你想在你的系统上本地运行这个应用程序吗？

要为本地开发设置前端，请执行以下提到的每个步骤：

  1. 安装运行此应用程序所需的所有依赖项。
  2. 从https://appwrite.io/在您的系统上安装 appwrite。
  3. 然后在 src/utils/config.js 文件的 'endpoint' 变量中设置你的 appwrite 的本地域名。
  4. 通过本地 appwrite 中的 localhost:port 创建一个项目。
  5. 并获取项目 ID 并将其保存到 src/utils/config.js 的“项目”中。
  6. 并通过 localhost:port 在您的 appwrite 中创建一些数据库集合。 集合必须如下所示

  1. 将 UsersInfo 集合的集合 ID 放在 src/utils/config.js 的 collectionID 中。
  2. 并将 Dev-Credential 的集合 ID 放在 src/utils/config.js 的 devCredential 中。
  3. 并在最后一次运行npm start在根文件夹中。

原文:

InfoAbout.me is an web application which provides convenience to users to check uploded contents by themselves on various social media platforms like Twitter and Dev.

The main motive of this application is to let the users to check their own uploaded contents on various social media platforms using this single application, that's why the application is named as InfoAbout.me.

Most of the time people all around the globe have to visit to various applications or social media platforms in a day to check what they have uploaded on social media platforms,so by keeping this problem in mind I made this web application where one can check uploaded contents by him/her on twitter and Dev using this single applcation.

Because of time limitation I integrated only two applications (Twitter + Dev) simple features in it, so using InfoAbout.me user can only see their uploaded contents, can't perform any operation with their uploaded contents.

In future this application will be enhanced by me and appwrite services like auth2 etc will also be implemented in it.

For now this application lets a user to login with InfoAbout.me, if user don't have an account then user will need to create an account.

After successfully Login user will need to authorize InfoAbout.me application with Twitter and Dev so that infoAbout.me can fetch User Data from these applications successfully.

Submission Category:

Web2 Wizards

Link to Code

This project was bootstrapped with Create React App, using the Redux and Redux Toolkit template.

Do you want to run this application locally on your system ?

To setup Frontend for local development go through the each step that mentioned below:

  1. Install all dependencies that are neccessary to run this application.
  2. Install appwrite on your system from https://appwrite.io/.
  3. then set your appwrite's local domain name in 'endpoint' variable of src/utils/config.js file.
  4. Create a project by going through the localhost:port in your appwrite locally.
  5. and Get a project Id and save it to 'project' of src/utils/config.js.
  6. and create few database collections in your appwrite by going through the localhost:port. collections must look like as shown below

  1. Put collection ID of UsersInfo collection in collectionID of src/utils/config.js.
  2. and also put collection ID of Dev-Credential in devCredential of src/utils/config.js.
  3. and at the last run npm start in the root folder.

Items.Title: 事件风暴 | Event Storming
Items.Description:
永远不会过时

  • 多米尼奥相关专家
  • 阿祖尔 - Comandos

    • 代表 o que aconteceu no sistema para o eventto ter sido gerado

    阿马雷洛 - 角色

    • 管理员、前任等...

    佛得角 - 达多斯

    • 例如： para um banco aprovar uma conta é feita uma pesquisa por varios dados dos 客户

    例如： quando a conta for aprovada envie um email

  • 编年史

    事件源头

    • 外部系统
    • 集体形式

      关键事件

      São eventos que levam de uma zona para outra, por exemplo: se eu tenho um site que precisa de assinatura para acessar o evento pivotal é a assinatura ser aprovada pois esse evento vai levar da zona de assinaturas para a zona de acesso ao site São eventos que levam de uma zona para outra, por exemplo: se eu tenho um site que precisa de assinatura para acessar o eventto pivotal é a assinatura ser aprovada pois esse eventto vai levar da zona de assinaturas para a zona de acesso ao site



    • 原文:

    Técnica criada por Alberto Brandolini que ajuda a entender de forma mais clara o domínio das aplicações através dos eventos gerados por elas.

    Normalmente acontece em forma de workshop. É uma dinâmica de grupo envolvendo domain experts e a área técnica.

    Ele é feito usando post its de cores diferentes como podemos ver na imagem, cada cor tem um significado

    Mapeamento dos eventos

    Laranja - Evento de dominio

    • verbo sempre no passado
    • relevante para experts de domínio
    • pode ser representado por um postit maior para representar o final de uma fase

    Azul - Comandos

    • representa o que aconteceu no sistema para o evento ter sido gerado

    Amarelo - Persona

    • admin, fornecedor, etc...

    Verde - Dados

    • dados necessários para tomar decisões
    • ex: para um banco aprovar uma conta é feita uma pesquisa por varios dados dos clientes

    Roxo - Policy

    • Quando X acontecer faça Y
    • ex: quando a conta for aprovada envie um email
    • pode ser feito manualmente ou automaticamente

    Cronologia

    Serve para definir a ordem dos eventos que podem inclusive acontecer de forma pararela

    Origem dos eventos

    • pode ser uma ação iniciada por um usuário
    • pode vir de um sistema externo
    • pode ser resultado do tempo passando
    • ou pode ser consequência de um outro evento

    Formação de agregados

    Ajuda a visualizar a formação de agregados e ter noção do tamanho desse agregado

    Eventos pivotais

    São eventos que levam de uma zona para outra, por exemplo: se eu tenho um site que precisa de assinatura para acessar o evento pivotal é a assinatura ser aprovada pois esse evento vai levar da zona de assinaturas para a zona de acesso ao site

Items.Title: 使用 Terraform 从头开始​​ Azure AKS 群集 | Azure AKS cluster from scratch using Terraform
Items.Description:
在使用 Terraform 在 Azure IaC 上创建培训材料时，我创建了一个小型演示项目来从头开始创建 Azure AKS 集群并将其与Terraform Cloud集成。 结果非常好，因此我想我会与更多的观众分享🙂。

详细说明可以在 repo README 中找到。 你需要自己的 Azure 帐户和订阅。 带有 Ubuntu 操作系统的节点

  • 分离 vnet 和子网
  • 启用自动缩放的单节点池
  • 系统分配的托管标识
  • 启用集群自动升级

    • 关于 Terraform Cloud 的快速说明 - 如果您还没有尝试过，那么您应该这样做。 它为最多 5 人的小型团队提供免费计划，并提供一种远程安全地管理状态的方法。

    快乐编码🤘。

    丹尼斯拉夫·加夫里洛夫👨‍🚀🤖🤷‍♂️ - Apr 23 4月23日

    Davide 'CoderDave' Benvegnù - Davide 'CoderDave' Benvegnù - May 10 5月10日



    原文:

    While working on creating training material on Azure IaC using Terraform, I created a small demo project to create an Azure AKS cluster from scratch and integrated it with Terraform Cloud. It came out quite nicely and therefore I thought I would share it with larger audience 🙂.

    Detailed instructions can be found on the repo README. You'll need your own Azure account and a subscription. The AKS cluster code is a local module and the created cluster will have the following features:

    • Nodes with Ubuntu OS
    • Azure CNI
    • Separate vnet and subnet
    • Single nodepool with autoscaling enabled
    • AKS managed Azure AD integration
    • System-assigned managed Identity
    • Cluster auto-upgrade enabled

    Quick note about Terraform Cloud - if you haven't tried it yet, you should. It has a free plan for smaller teams up to 5 and provides a way to manage state remotely and securely.

    Happy coding 🤘.

    Denislav Gavrilov 👨‍🚀🤖🤷‍♂️ - Apr 23

    Davide 'CoderDave' Benvegnù - May 10

Items.Title: 对 DevOps 的职业感到好奇？ 如何准备好面试 | Curious about a Career in DevOps? How to Prep for Strong Interviews
Items.Description:
那些决定从事DevOps职业的人并不总是知道自己想做什么。 他们甚至可能不知道系统管理员或 DevOps 专家的基本职责。

基本上，DevOps 专家为软件开发周期带来了秩序。 他们创建流程和方法来平衡需求，并可能提供帮助的工具。 它们促进了从编码和部署到更新和维护的整个开发生命周期。

在我们的领域中，独立搜索缺失信息和不断学习新事物的能力是必不可少且有价值的。

在过去的四五年里，它引起了人们的兴趣。 从本质上讲，它是关于 IT 为 IT 的。 从这个角度来看，任务通常与自动化、改进、优化或创建新事物有关。

因此，如果您对这个职业感兴趣，请准备好不断学习新事物并跟上 DevOps 世界的最新动态。

我们的专家必须是多才多艺的，因为 DevOps 技术堆栈不严重依赖开发技术。 他们可以从事各种项目，因为 DevOps 原则是通用的。

随着 IT 行业不断扩展系统并迁移到云服务，这些项目对 DevOps 的需求正在迅速增加。 同时，由于该领域是新领域，因此没有太多经验丰富的专业人士。

例如，我们的团队现在需要大约 20 名 DevOps 专家。

我们在 DevOps 简历上要注意什么？

  • 我们在简历中寻找的第一件事是稳定性——候选人在特定团队工作的时间。

  • 我们还考虑了专家在实践中熟悉的技术。 我们必须知道一个人对某些技术的理解程度以及他们是否可以被认为是这些技术的专家。

  • 我们团队中的 DevOps 专家经常与外国客户和客户端开发人员进行交流。 因此，候选人应该能说流利的英语，以便有效地进行交流。

在学习如何自动化基础架构设置、部署应用程序及其工作区以及履行相关职责之前，您必须能够手动创建基础架构。

这是完全理解那里的事情如何运作的唯一方法。

我们考虑多种因素。 我们没有可以立即做出决定的控制问题。 候选人是否有足够的技能或需要额外的培训通常在面试结束时很清楚。

令人惊讶的是，最简单、最基本的问题往往是候选人无法回答的。 很少有人意识到从互联网上获得现成解决方案的频率有多高。 在我们看来，这表明了专业兴趣和可培训性。

即使是这样的基本问题也常常难倒候选人：

  • 如何在控制台中重命名文件？

  • 我在哪里查看 Linux 中的日志？

  • 如何在存储库中找到包？

相关：想要提高技能？ 聘请的合作伙伴可以提供认证、训练营、课程等方面的帮助！

基本 DevOp 专家技能：你需要知道的

  • 用户权限（操作系统用户权限）、组和访问权限设置
  • 文字处理、文件管理、进程和服务管理、网络、计时、磁盘管理、bash 基础
  • 具有 Centos/Ubuntu 包管理器的经验，从源代码安装包
  • 网络的原理
  • 安装和配置nginx/apache web服务器的实用技巧
  • 有安装 WordPress、Joomla 和 Drupal 等流行 CMS 的经验
  • 邮件的原理和邮件协议的目的
  • 有配置postfix/exim+dovecot+roundcube的经验
  • 作为 DevOps 专家，我是否需要知道如何编写代码？

    不必知道如何编写代码。 但是，对于自动化管理任务（例如部署应用程序、配置其必须运行的环境或配置整个基础架构）来说，这是一项宝贵的技能。 在这种情况下，DevOps 编码技能无疑会很有用。

    您是否正在寻找 DevOps 或其他技术子角色（如工程或数据分析师）的新角色？ 准备 DevOps 专家面试的有用资源

    使用终端

  • 关于 Linux 的文章

目录结构：

  • 为初学者解释的 Linux 目录结构

权限：

包管理：

网络如何运作：

  • 常见问题
  • 网络服务器配置：

    数据库：

    • 数据库
    • 数据库管理系统

    邮件：

    • 如何在 Ubuntu 18.04 上使用 Webmail 配置 Postfix

    他与 AWS 和 Linux 合作，并在Nix United的团队环境中培训新人。



原文:

Hired welcomes guest contributor Vyacheslav Nikitan, the Lead DevOps Trainer at NIX United, to share insights for those interested in a career as a DevOps specialist.

Those who decide to pursue a career in DevOps do not always know what they want to do. They may not even be aware of the fundamental responsibilities of a systems administrator or a DevOps specialist.

Basically, a DevOps specialist brings order to the software development cycle. They create processes and methodologies to balance needs and may surface tools to help. They facilitate the entire development lifespan from coding and deployment, to updates and maintenance.

In our field, the ability to independently search for missing information and constantly learn new things is essential and valuable.

DevOps is a relatively new discipline. It has piqued people's interest for the past four or five years. Essentially, it is about IT for the sake of IT. DevOps specialists are involved in the automation and optimization of IT processes.

Tasks are frequently related to automation, improvement, optimization, or the creation of something new from this perspective.

The direction of DevOps is toward modern technologies and approaches with diverse applications and utilities. So, if you're interested in this career, be prepared to constantly learn new things and keep up with what's going on in the DevOps world.

Our specialists must be versatile because the DevOps technology stack does not rely heavily on development technologies. They can work on various projects because DevOps principles are universal.

As the IT industry continues to expand systems and migrate to cloud services, the demand for DevOps on these projects is increasing rapidly. At the same time, because the field is new, there aren't many experienced professionals available.

For example, our team now requires approximately 20 DevOps specialists.

What do we pay attention to on a DevOps resume?

  • The first thing we look for in a resume is stability — how long the candidate has been with a particular team.

  • We also consider the technology that the specialist is familiar with in practice. We must know how well a person understands certain technologies and whether they can be considered an expert in them.

  • DevOps specialists in our teams frequently communicate with foreign customers and client-side developers. As an outcome, the candidate should be fluent in English in order to communicate effectively.

Common interview topics and questions for DevOps specialists

You must be able to manually create infrastructure before learning how to automate infrastructure setup, deploy applications and their workspaces, and fulfill related duties.

That's the only way to fully comprehend how things work out there.

We consider a variety of factors. We don't have control questions on which we make an immediate decision. Whether a candidate has sufficient skills or requires additional training is usually clear at the end of the interview.

Surprisingly, the simplest, most basic questions are frequently unanswerable by candidates. Few people realize how often ready-made solutions are obtained from the Internet. This is an indication of professional interest and trainability, in our opinion.

Even such basic questions often stump candidates:

  • How do I rename a file in the console?

  • Where do I look at logs in Linux?

  • How do you find a package in a repository?

Related: Looking to skill up? Hired partners can help with certifications, bootcamps, courses, and more!

Basic DevOp Specialist skills: what you need to know

  • The general OS structure and purpose of Linux directories
  • User permissions (OS user rights), groups, and access rights setup
  • Word processing, file management, process and service management, networking, timing, disk management, bash basics
  • Experience with Centos/Ubuntu package managers, installing packages from source
  • The principles of the web
  • Practical skills in installing and configuring nginx/apache web servers
  • Experience in installing popular CMSs such as WordPress, Joomla, and Drupal
  • The purpose, operating principles, and organization of DBMS MySQL, SQL queries, database backup and restore
  • The principles of mail and the purpose of mail protocols
  • Experience in configuring postfix/exim+dovecot+roundcube
  • The principles of DNS and the types of resource records and their purposes

Do I need to know how to write code for a career as a DevOps Specialist?

It is not necessary to know how to write code. However, it is a valuable skill for automating administrative tasks such as deploying an application, configuring the environment in which it must run, or configuring an entire infrastructure. DevOps coding skills will undoubtedly be useful in such situations.

Are you looking for a new role in DevOps or another tech subrole like engineering or data analyst? Complete a free profile and let employers come to you!

Useful resources to prepare for a DevOps Specialist interview

Linux basics:

  • Linux Tutorial
  • Using the Terminal
  • Articles about Linux

Directory structure:

  • Linux Directory Structure Explained for Beginners

Permissions:

Package management:

How the web works:

  • Common questions
  • How the Web works

Web servers configuration:

Databases:

  • Database
  • Database Management Systems

Mail:

  • How to Configure Postfix with Webmail on Ubuntu 18.04

Vyacheslav Nikitan’s IT experience includes nine years as a system administrator and DevOps specialist. He works with AWS and Linux and trains newcomers in team settings at Nix United.
Items.Author: hired

Items.Title: 关于 Youtube 的不喜欢按钮的回顾 | Retrospectives on the dislike button of Youtube
Items.Description:
在 2021 年初进行的实验之后，Youtube 宣布从其视频托管平台上删除“我不喜欢”计数器。 旨在保护摄像师的更改。
然后在 2021 年 11 月 10 日，这家视频托管巨头宣布在其平台上移除“我不喜欢”内容的柜台。 该公司的决定是为了保护其创作者免受骚扰和某些有针对性的攻击。

最初，在 2021 年初的实验期间，不喜欢计数器已被暂时删除。

最后，您仍然可以通过按下拇指来表示您不喜欢某个视频。 但该号码不再公开显示。 另一方面，创作者总是可以在他们的仪表板中看到它，以了解他们的频道上哪些有效，哪些无效。

保留保留内容创建者

该公司解释说，是先前实验的结果导致了计数器的拆除。 根据 Youtube 的声明，数据显示，这次取消计数器，虽然让用户有机会使用“我不喜欢”按钮，但导致点击该按钮的用户减少，只是为了伤害创作者。 小渠道显然特别容易受到攻击的恶意行为。 这样，骚扰的风险就会降低。

提醒一下，YouTube上最“不喜欢”的视频是平台自己制作的视频！ 我的结论

首先，我和公司的观点是一致的。 他补充说，该决定是“我们为继续保护创作者免受骚扰而采取的众多步骤之一”。 目标是在其平台上培养尊重、安全和包容性，该平台的算法有时被指控提供暴力和攻击性视频。

其次，作为 YouTube 用户，不喜欢的次数是决定我是否可以观看视频的决定因素。 用户不再将这一标准视为内容质量的保证。

总之，巨头 Youtube 的决定当然有缺点，但也有完全正当的优势。 事实证明，尽管每月有 20 亿用户，我们还是很快接受了公司的这种或多或少激进的变化。

奖金

对于所有不想遵循公司当前决定的边缘人😅，您仍然可以自定义您的 Youtube 频道以显示视频的不喜欢数量。 在该说明上，我建议您遵循本教程：

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=T5NEsBVqY28 https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=T5NEsBVqY28



原文:

Following experiments conducted earlier in 2021, Youtube had announced the removal of the "I don't like" counter from its video hosting platform. A change that aimed to protect videographers.
Then on November 10, 2021 the video hosting giant had announced the removal of the counter of "I do not like" content on its platform. A decision that the company had justified by the desire to preserve its creators from harassment and certain targeted attacks.

Initially the dislikes counter had already been temporarily removed during experiments, earlier in 2021.

Finally it is still possible to show that you don't like a video by pressing the thumb down. But the number is no longer displayed publicly. On the other hand, creators can always see it in their dashboard to get an idea of what works and what doesn't on their channel.

PRESERVING RESERVING CONTENT CREATORS

The company had explained that it was the results of a previous experiment that led to this removal of the counter. According to the statement of Youtube, the data showed that this removal of the counter, while leaving users the opportunity to use the "I don't like" button, had led to a decrease in users who clicked on this button only to harm the creators. Malicious acts to which small channels were apparently particularly exposed. In this way, the risks of harassment will be reduced.

As a reminder, the most "disliked" video on YouTube is a video produced by the platform itself! The YouTube Rewind of 2018 has more than 15 million dislikes!

MY CONCLUSION

First of all, I share the same opinion as the company. YouTube said that "we've heard in the course of the experiment that some of you have used the red thumbs counter to decide whether to watch a video or not. We know you might not agree with this decision, but we think it's the right thing to do for the platform." The decision is "one of many steps we are taking to continue to protect creators from harassment," he added. The goal is to foster respect, safety and inclusiveness on its platform, whose algorithm is sometimes accused of offering violent and offensive videos.

Secondly, as a YouTube user, the number of Dislikes was a determining factor in deciding whether or not I could watch a video. This criterion can no longer be taken into account by users as a guarantee of quality or not of a content.

In conclusion, the decision of the giant Youtube certainly had disadvantages but with perfectly justifiable advantages. The proof, despite the 2 billion monthly users, we have rather quickly accepted this more or less radical change of the company.

BONUS

For all the fringe people who don't want to follow the current decision of the company 😅, you can still customize your Youtube channel to display the number of dislikes of a video. On that note I suggest you follow this tutorial:

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=T5NEsBVqY28
Items.Author: jocelyn10

Items.Title: 用于 Azure 静态 Web 应用上的无头 CMS 的 Blazor WebAssembly | Blazor WebAssembly for Headless CMS on Azure Static Web Apps
Items.Description:
构建静态网站最流行的场景之一是为我自己或我的组织运行博客网站。 现在，您想将您的 WordPress 博客网站迁移到静态网站，但这看起来并不容易。

如果您仍想使用 WordPress 网站编写内容但只想刷新网站外的 UI 怎么办？ 如果您甚至可以通过Blazor WebAssembly使用 C# 怎么办？ 您现在可以使用现有的 WordPress 站点作为真实数据源，并使用您喜欢的方法在单独的实例中构建 UI。 这听起来很有吸引力吗？

在这篇文章中，我将讨论如何将服务的 WordPress 实例用作无头 CMS，构建 Blazor WebAssembly 应用程序以利用 WordPress 作为数据源，并将其托管到Azure 静态 Web 应用程序。

您可以从此 GitHub 存储库下载示例应用程序代码：

服务的 WordPress 站点实例

我有一个不再维护但存档的服务 WordPress 站点。

为了将此站点用作数据源，我需要能够通过 API 访问内容。 幸运的是，WordPress 提供了完整的 HTTP API 端点。 在终端中输入以下命令。

 curl -X GET https://public-api.wordpress.com/rest/v1.1/sites/<site-name>.wordpress.com/posts/

或者，您可以使用您喜欢的 UI 工具（如 Postman）来获取内容列表：

你得到内容。 您现在需要做的就是在 Blazore WASM 应用程序上呈现内容。 有两种方法：

  1. 直接从 Blazor WASM 应用调用 API，或
  2. 通过代理 API 应用从 Blazor WASM 应用调用 API

两种方法都有优点和缺点，但让我们用第二种方法来实现。

代理 API 应用程序

我们不需要Azure Static Web Apps的代理 API 应用程序。 但是，如果您需要克服 CORS 问题、处理各种 API 或处理一些安全问题，使用代理 API 是一个不错的选择。 因为 Azure 静态 Web 应用程序原生支持这个代理 API 功能，所以我们可以简单地使用它。 首先创建一个Azure Functions应用的HTTP 触发器。

为 WordPress API 端点定义GetPosts并创建一个HttpClient实例。

 public static class PostHttpTrigger { private const string GetPosts = "https://public-api.wordpress.com/rest/v1.1/sites/{0}/posts" ; private static HttpClient http = new HttpClient ();

如果将OpenAPI 扩展添加到函数应用，则 Blazor WASM 应用可以轻松访问此代理 API。 我将在本文后面处理这部分。

 [ FunctionName ( "PostHttpTrigger" )] [ OpenApiOperation ( operationId : "posts.get" , tags : new [] { "posts" })] [ OpenApiResponseWithBody ( statusCode : HttpStatusCode . OK , contentType : "application/json" , bodyType : typeof ( PostCollection ), Description = "The OK response" )] public static async Task < IActionResult > GetPostsAsync ( [ HttpTrigger ( AuthorizationLevel . Anonymous , "GET" , Route = "posts" )] HttpRequest req , ILogger log ) {

从环境变量中获取实际的 WordPress 站点名称。

 var requestUri = new Uri ( string . Format ( GetPosts , Environment . GetEnvironmentVariable ( "SITE__NAME" )));

最后，调用 WordPress API 并将结果反序列PostCollection对象。

 var json = await http . GetStringAsync ( requestUri ). ConfigureAwait ( false ); var posts = JsonConvert . DeserializeObject < PostCollection >( json ); return new OkObjectResult ( posts ); } }

下面是PostCollection类及其子类的定义。 尽管 WordPress API 返回的结果种类繁多，但我们只需要在PostCollection类中定义其中的一些。

 public class PostCollection { public virtual int Found { get ; set ; } public virtual List < PostItem > Posts { get ; set ; } [ JsonProperty ( "meta" )] public virtual Metadata Metadata { get ; set ; } } public class PostItem { [ JsonProperty ( "ID" )] public virtual int PostId { get ; set ; } public virtual Author Author { get ; set ; } [ JsonProperty ( "date" )] public virtual DateTimeOffset DatePublished { get ; set ; } public virtual string Title { get ; set ; } [ JsonProperty ( "URL" )] public virtual string Url { get ; set ; } public virtual string Excerpt { get ; set ; } public virtual string Content { get ; set ; } } public class Author { [ JsonProperty ( "ID" )] public virtual int AuthorId { get ; set ; } [ JsonProperty ( "first_name" )] public virtual string FirstName { get ; set ; } [ JsonProperty ( "last_name" )] public virtual string Surname { get ; set ; } public virtual string Name { get ; set ; } } public class Metadata { public virtual Dictionary < string , string > Links { get ; set ; } [ JsonProperty ( "next_page" )] public virtual string NextPage { get ; set ; } [ JsonProperty ( "wpcom" )] public virtual bool IsWordpressCom { get ; set ; } }

最后，配置local.settings.json文件。 我们将它设置为这个文件，就像我们之前提到的SITE__NAME的环境变量一样。 除此之外，我们为 Blazor WebAssembly 应用程序集成设置了 CORS。

 { "IsEncrypted" : false , "Values" : { "AzureWebJobsStorage" : "UseDevelopmentStorage=true" , "FUNCTIONS_WORKER_RUNTIME" : "dotnet" , "SITE__NAME" : "<site-name>.wordpress.com" }, "Host" : { "CORS" : "*" }
}

可以了，好了！ 运行代理 API 应用程序，您将看到如下结果：

此代理 API 应用程序的 OpenAPI 文档 URL 是以下之一：

  • 打开Visual Studio并创建一个新的 Blazor WebAssembly 项目。

    将其命名为WebApp ，并将其余部分保留为默认值。 创建应用程序后，按 F5 键运行应用程序，您将看到如下屏幕：

    是时候将代理 API 连接到此 Blazor 应用了。 首先，确保代理 API 应用程序在后台运行。 然后，单击“连接的服务”菜单。

    然后点击“➕”按钮。

    选择“ OpenAPI ”选项作为服务参考。

    输入 OpenAPI URL，给出ProxyClient的命名空间和WebApp.Proxies的类名，然后单击“完成”按钮。

    你会看到代理 API 已添加到 Blazor 应用程序中。

    单击右侧的三个点，然后选择“查看生成的代码”菜单。 你能看到 OpenAPI 文档中自动生成的代码吗？

    我们只需要使用这段代码。 首先，打开Program.cs文件，添加ProxyClient实例的依赖。

    在本地计算机上开发此应用程序时，默认的基本 URL 是http://localhost:7071/api 。 但是一旦它部署到 Azure，它必须遵循实际的主机地址（第 8-12 行）。

     builder . Services . AddScoped ( sp => new HttpClient { BaseAddress = new Uri ( builder . HostEnvironment . BaseAddress ) }); // Add these lines to inject the dependency of ProxyClient builder . Services . AddScoped ( sp => { var client = sp . GetService < HttpClient >(); var api = new ProxyClient ( client ); if (! builder . HostEnvironment . IsDevelopment ()) { var baseUrl = $" { builder . HostEnvironment . BaseAddress . TrimEnd ( '/' )} /api" ; api . BaseUrl = baseUrl ; } return api ; });

    到目前为止，您的 Blazor 应用程序现在可以免费访问代理 API。 所以，让我们来实现这部分。 首先，我们简单地修改index.razor页面，并在这篇文章中呈现博客文章列表。

    注入通过Program.cs添加的ProxyClient的依赖。

     @page "/" @ * Inject ProxyClient dependency * @ @using WebApp . Proxies @inject ProxyClient Api

    在处理博客文章列表的<SurveyPrompt>组件下方添加 HTML 点点滴滴。

     ... < SurveyPrompt Title = "How is Blazor working for you?" /> @if ( posts == null ) { < p >< em > Loading ...</ em ></ p > } else { < table class =" table "> < thead > < tr > < th > Date </ th > < th > Author </ th > < th > Title </ th > < th > Excerpt </ th > </ tr > </ thead > < tbody > @foreach ( var post in posts ) { < tr > < td > @post . Date . ToString ()</ td > < td > @post . Author . Name </ td > < td >< a href = "@post.URL" > @post . Title </ a ></ td > < td > @ (( MarkupString ) post . Excerpt )</ td > </ tr > } </ tbody > </ table > }

    这是调用代理 API 的实际代码。 通过此代理 API，此页面接收博客文章列表。

     @code { private List < PostItem > posts ; protected override async Task OnInitializedAsync () { var collection = await Api . Posts_getAsync (). ConfigureAwait ( false ); posts = collection ?. Posts . ToList (); } }

    完成！ 再次运行 Blazor 应用。 您可以看到从 Wordpress 填充的博客文章列表。

    您需要将所有代码推送到 GitHub 存储库。

    所有编码部分已完成！ 现在是时候将应用程序发布到Azure 静态 Web 应用程序了。 我不打算介绍这部分。 相反，这里是我之前关于部署的帖子的链接。

    • 部署静态 Web 应用程序

    使用代理 API 应用完成 Blazor WebAssembly 应用的发布后，请访问网站以确认一切是否正常。

    到目前为止，我已经介绍了如何使用Azure Functions代理 API 构建Blazor WebAssembly应用程序，并将服务的 WordPress 站点用作数据源。 此外，我已将此应用程序发布到Azure 静态 Web 应用程序实例。 虽然这篇文章只是实现了高级概念，但它基本上已经触及了你需要的几乎所有东西。 因此，您可以使用尽可能多的 API 端点并使 UI 更漂亮。 然后，您仍然可以使用现有的 WordPress 网站作为您的真实数据源。



原文:

One of the most popular scenarios to build a static website is to run a blog site for myself or my organisation. WordPress is the most popular service for this purpose. Now, you want to migrate your WordPress blog site to a static website, but it doesn't look easy.

What if you still want to use the WordPress site to write content but only want to refresh the UI outside the site? What if you can even use C# for it through Blazor WebAssembly? You are now able to use the existing WordPress site as the data source of truth and build a UI in a separate instance with your preferred method. Does that sound attractive?

Throughout this post, I'm going to discuss how to use the serviced WordPress instance as the headless CMS, build the Blazor WebAssembly app to make use of the WordPress as a data source, and host it to Azure Static Web Apps.

You can download the sample app code from this GitHub repository:

Serviced WordPress Site Instance

I've got a serviced WordPress site that is no longer maintained but archived.

In order to use this site as a data source, I need to be able to access to the contents through APIs. Fortunately, WordPress provides full of HTTP API endpoints. Enter the following command in your terminal.

curl -X GET https://public-api.wordpress.com/rest/v1.1/sites/<site-name>.wordpress.com/posts/

Alternatively, you can use your preferred UI tool like Postman to get the list of contents:

You get the contents. All you need to do now is to render the contents on the Blazore WASM app. There are two approaches to it:

  1. Directly call the API from the Blazor WASM app, or
  2. Call the API from the Blazor WASM app through the proxy API app

Both approaches have pros and cons, but let's make it with the second approach.

Proxy API Application

We don't need the proxy API app for Azure Static Web Apps. However, if you need to overcome the CORS issue, handle various APIs, or deal with some security concerns, using the proxy API is a good option. Because Azure Static Web Apps natively supports this proxy API feature, we can simply use it. First of all create an HTTP trigger of Azure Functions app.

Define GetPosts for the WordPress API endpoint and create an HttpClient instance.

public static class PostHttpTrigger
{ private const string GetPosts = "https://public-api.wordpress.com/rest/v1.1/sites/{0}/posts"; private static HttpClient http = new HttpClient();

If you add the OpenAPI extension to your function app, your Blazor WASM app can easily access this proxy API. I'll handle this part later in this post.

 [FunctionName("PostHttpTrigger")] [OpenApiOperation(operationId: "posts.get", tags: new[] { "posts" })] [OpenApiResponseWithBody(statusCode: HttpStatusCode.OK, contentType: "application/json", bodyType: typeof(PostCollection), Description = "The OK response")] public static async Task<IActionResult> GetPostsAsync( [HttpTrigger(AuthorizationLevel.Anonymous, "GET", Route = "posts")] HttpRequest req, ILogger log) {

Get the actual WordPress site name from the environment variables.

 var requestUri = new Uri(string.Format(GetPosts, Environment.GetEnvironmentVariable("SITE__NAME")));

Finally, call the WordPress API and deserialise the result to the PostCollection object.

 var json = await http.GetStringAsync(requestUri).ConfigureAwait(false); var posts = JsonConvert.DeserializeObject<PostCollection>(json); return new OkObjectResult(posts); }
}

Here are the definitions of the PostCollection class and its subclasses. Although the WordPress API returns a massive variety of results, we only need some of them defined in the PostCollection class.

public class PostCollection
{ public virtual int Found { get; set; } public virtual List<PostItem> Posts { get; set; } [JsonProperty("meta")] public virtual Metadata Metadata { get; set; }
} public class PostItem
{ [JsonProperty("ID")] public virtual int PostId { get; set; } public virtual Author Author { get; set; } [JsonProperty("date")] public virtual DateTimeOffset DatePublished { get; set; } public virtual string Title { get; set; } [JsonProperty("URL")] public virtual string Url { get; set; } public virtual string Excerpt { get; set; } public virtual string Content { get; set; }
} public class Author
{ [JsonProperty("ID")] public virtual int AuthorId { get; set; } [JsonProperty("first_name")] public virtual string FirstName { get; set; } [JsonProperty("last_name")] public virtual string Surname { get; set; } public virtual string Name { get; set; }
} public class Metadata
{ public virtual Dictionary<string, string> Links { get; set; } [JsonProperty("next_page")] public virtual string NextPage { get; set; } [JsonProperty("wpcom")] public virtual bool IsWordpressCom { get; set; }
}

Finally, configure the local.settings.json file. We set it to this file as we previously referred to the environment variable of SITE__NAME. In addition to that, we set the CORS for the Blazor WebAssembly app integration.

{ "IsEncrypted": false, "Values": { "AzureWebJobsStorage": "UseDevelopmentStorage=true", "FUNCTIONS_WORKER_RUNTIME": "dotnet", "SITE__NAME": "<site-name>.wordpress.com" }, "Host": { "CORS": "*" }
}

All set! Run the proxy API app, and you will see the result like below:

The OpenAPI document URL to this proxy API app is one of the following:

  • http://localhost:7071/api/swagger.json – OpenAPI V2
  • http://localhost:7071/api/openapi/v2.json – OpenAPI V2
  • http://localhost:7071/api/openapi/v3.json – OpenAPI V3

Blazor WebAssembly Application

Open Visual Studio and create a new Blazor WebAssembly project.

Give it the name of WebApp, and leave the rest as default. Once the app is created, run the app by typing the F5 key, and you will see the screen like this:

It's time to connect to the proxy API to this Blazor app. First, make sure that the proxy API app is running background. Then, click the "Connected Services" menu.

Then click the "➕" button.

Choose the "OpenAPI" option as a service reference.

Enter the OpenAPI URL, give the namespace of WebApp.Proxies and class name of ProxyClient, and click the "Finish" button.

You'll see the proxy API is added to the Blazor app.

Click the three dots on the right-hand side and select the "View generated code" menu. Can you see the auto-generated code from the OpenAPI document?

We just need to consume this code. First, open the Program.cs file and add the dependency of the ProxyClient instance.

While developing this app on your local machine, the default base URL is http://localhost:7071/api. But once it's deployed to Azure, it must follow the actual host address (line #8-12).

builder.Services.AddScoped(sp => new HttpClient { BaseAddress = new Uri(builder.HostEnvironment.BaseAddress) }); // Add these lines to inject the dependency of ProxyClient
builder.Services.AddScoped(sp =>
{ var client = sp.GetService<HttpClient>(); var api = new ProxyClient(client); if (!builder.HostEnvironment.IsDevelopment()) { var baseUrl = $"{builder.HostEnvironment.BaseAddress.TrimEnd('/')}/api"; api.BaseUrl = baseUrl; } return api;
});

By this far, your Blazor app is now freely accessible to the proxy API. So, let's implement this part. First, we simply modify the index.razor page and render the list of blog posts in this post.

Inject the dependency of ProxyClient added through Program.cs.

@page "/" @* Inject ProxyClient dependency *@
@using WebApp.Proxies
@inject ProxyClient Api

Add the HTML bits and pieces just below the <SurveyPrompt> component that handles the list of blog posts.

...
<SurveyPrompt Title="How is Blazor working for you?" /> @if (posts == null)
{ <p><em>Loading...</em></p>
}
else
{ <table class="table">
 <thead> <tr> <th>Date</th> <th>Author</th> <th>Title</th> <th>Excerpt</th> </tr> </thead> <tbody> @foreach (var post in posts) { <tr> <td>@post.Date.ToString()</td> <td>@post.Author.Name</td> <td><a href="@post.URL">@post.Title</a></td> <td>@((MarkupString)post.Excerpt)</td> </tr> } </tbody> </table>
}

Here's the actual code to call the proxy API. Through this proxy API, this page receives the list of blog posts.

@code { private List<PostItem> posts; protected override async Task OnInitializedAsync() { var collection = await Api.Posts_getAsync().ConfigureAwait(false); posts = collection?.Posts.ToList(); }
}

It's done! Run the Blazor app again. You can see the list of blog posts populated from Wordpress.

You need to push all the code to the GitHub repository.

Azure Static Web Apps Hosting

All coding parts have been completed! Now it's time to publish the app to Azure Static Web Apps. I'm not going to walk through this part. Instead, here are links to my previous posts about the deployment.

  • Deploying Static Web Apps

Once you complete publishing your Blazor WebAssembly app with the proxy API app, go to the website to confirm whether everything is OK or not.

So far, I've walked through how to build a Blazor WebAssembly app with an Azure Functions proxy API and use serviced WordPress site as a data source. Also, I've published this app to Azure Static Web Apps instance. Although this post has only implemented the high-level concepts, it has basically touched almost everything you need. Therefore, it's up to you to use as many API endpoints as possible and make the UI prettier. Then, you can still use the existing WordPress site as your data source of truth.
Items.Author: justinyoo

Items.Title: Las Tecnologías de la Información y Comunicación (TIC) en la educación | Las Tecnologías de la Información y Comunicación (TIC) en la educación
Items.Description:
富恩特：（瓦莱，2018）

富恩特：（洛佩斯，2016）

结论, las Tecnologías de la Información y Comunicación (TIC)significan una herramienta muy importante para la adquisición de los aprendizajes, la experiencia y los datos estadísticos de los últimos años, así lo ratifican, por lo que cualquier método de enseñanza en Superiores no deben dejar de lado estas importantes herramientas y, por el contrario, se debe profundizar su utilización al máximo。

参考文献

先锋派。 洛杉矶先锋。 卢西亚洛佩兹_C。 蒙托亚·加戈，B. (2020)。 西班牙电信。 国家能源委员会。

康乃馨。 Obtenido de Sin las TIC, la educación superior y de posgrado no está completa: https://www.esan.edu.pe/conexion-esan/sin-las-tic-la-educacion-superior-y-de-posgrado-no-esta-completa Obtenido de Sin las TIC，la educación Superior y de posgrado no está completa： https ://www.esan.edu.pe/conexion-esan/sin-las-tic-la-educación-superior-y-de-posgrado- 没有完整的



原文:

Fuente: (Valle, 2018)

Hoy en días las tecnologías se encuentran en constante evolución, formando parte de nuestra vida desde hace varios años, de hecho, según un estudio del Instituto Nacional de Estadística y Censos del Ecuador (INEC), del 2019 al 2020 aumentó el porcentaje de personas que utilizan internet en su hogar a nivel nacional, pasaron del 68.1% al 86.1%. Hoy en día estas estadísticas se mantendrían o se elevarían (Peña M & Herrera, 2021).

Este avance es una realidad a la que la educación debe adaptarse y, por ello, si se desea que se desarrolle un cambio en las mentalidades de las personas, la enseñanza es la clave. Los modelos tradicionales de aprendizaje (mediante libros de texto, memorización continua de temas, enseñanza por pizarra, etc.) tienen que dar paso a nuevas herramientas. Tanto las personas como el mundo son diferentes, por ello, no se recomendaría enseñar siguiendo los mismos métodos de hace 10 años.

Al utilizar las TIC se han abierto más posibilidades, un claro ejemplo es la tecnología 5G (quinta generación de tecnologías de telefonía móvil), esta tecnología permite tener más dispositivos conectados y hoy en día es algo especialmente necesario, ya que casi 1.400 millones de personas utilizan Internet móvil, este número puede elevarse hasta 5.000 millones de personas que utilicen internet en sus dispositivos móviles en 2025. (La Vanguardia, 2019) No obstante, aunque el uso de las TIC es clave, se debe tener a consideración que la labor que cumplen los docentes en la sociedad es esencial. Pero aun así es necesario que el enfoque de la enseñanza siga avanzando en conjunto con las personas. A futuro la enseñanza de los docentes a los alumnos debe basarse en la interacción entre ellos, apoyándose en el uso de las TIC y logrando un mayor intercambio de conocimientos, dentro de un eficiente método de enseñanza.

Fuente: (López, 2016)

Ahora bien, con el pasar de los años puede que las personas tengan una mentalidad en la que se haya dado por sentado que los pilares de una buena educación se basen en la formalidad, exigencias y estrés (por ejemplo, al rendir un examen). Este método de enseñanza se desarrolla durante mucho tiempo, dando lugar a un complicado proceso de aprendizaje de las personas; la dificultad reside en que el cerebro relaciona el aprendizaje con emociones negativas. Por el contrario, si nos enfocamos en un método de aprendizaje del niño, ellos aprenden mediante juegos y es muy positivo tanto la enseñanza como los resultados, porque el cerebro relaciona el aprender con emociones alegres y por ello el proceso de aprendizaje es bueno. (Montoya Gago, 2020)

De manera más específica, si nos enfocamos en el método de aprendizaje de un universitario-adulto, ellos aprenden sintiendo una presión emotivo-cognitiva, por ejemplo, al no aprobar una materia, por lo que pasan en su mayoría estresados y frustrados, desarrollando así sentimientos y emociones negativas, las cuales son limitantes para la adquisición de los aprendizajes. Por esta razón, es importante crear un método de educación que desarrolle sentimientos y emociones positivas en las personas.

En conclusión, las Tecnologías de la Información y Comunicación (TIC) significan una herramienta muy importante para la adquisición de los aprendizajes, la experiencia y los datos estadísticos de los últimos años, así lo ratifican, por lo que cualquier método de enseñanza en los estudios superiores no deben dejar de lado estas importantes herramientas y, por el contrario, se debe profundizar su utilización al máximo.

Referencias

La Vanguardia. (2 de Octubre de 2019). LA VANGUARDIA. Obtenido de El 5G ya es una realidad, ¿cómo cambiará nuestra vida?: https://www.lavanguardia.com/tecnologia/20191002/47667791796/5g-realidad-como-cambiara-nuestra-vida.html#:~:text=El%205G%20permitirá%20tener%2C%20en,a%205.000%20millones%20en%202025.

López, L. (4 de Mayo de 2016). LUCIALOPEZ_C. Obtenido de Las TIC en la educación: https://www.lucialopezcuenca.com/blog/las-tic-en-la-educaci%C3%B3n

Montoya Gago, B. (2020). Telefónica. Obtenido de ¿Qué papel ocupan las TIC en la educación actual?: https://blogthinkbig.com/tic-educacion-digital-niall-ferguson

Peña M, A., & Herrera, L. (2021). Indicadores de tecnología de la información y comunicación. INEC.

Valle, Y. (23 de Agosto de 2018). conexionesan. Obtenido de Sin las TIC, la educación superior y de posgrado no está completa: https://www.esan.edu.pe/conexion-esan/sin-las-tic-la-educacion-superior-y-de-posgrado-no-esta-completa

Items.Title: 带有免费使用或开源的屏幕截图的最佳时间跟踪软件是什么？ | What is the best time tracking software with screenshots that's free to use or open source?
Items.Description:
带有免费使用或开源的屏幕截图的最佳时间跟踪软件是什么？

好吧，如果您直接与客户合作并希望提供工作证明和任务报告，我相信需要使用报告选项进行时间跟踪，如果每隔几分钟就有截图会很棒......

当然可以选择，但如果它是免费或开源的，那就太好了。

分享你的意见。

阿什瓦德·普拉丹—— May 12 5月12日



原文:

What is the best time tracking software with screenshots that's free to use or open source?

Well, if you are working with clients directly and want to provide proof of the work and task report, I believe that is required to have time tracking with report options, also it will be great if it have screenshots every few minutes...

Take f course optional but it will be great if it's free or open source.

Share your thoughts.

Ashirwad Pradhan - May 12

Items.Title: 这是一次很棒的经历，也是一个很棒的工具。 | What's the alternative to stumpleupon toolbar? it was a great experience and it was a great tool.
Items.Description:
这是一次很棒的经历，也是一个很棒的工具。

对于那些不知道的人，它是一个随机给你网站的工具，如果你觉得无聊并想随机上网，你可以点赞你喜欢和不喜欢的东西，这也可以帮助你为网站添加书签，更加虚拟书签方法。

蒂亚戈-席尔瓦-洛佩斯May 11 5月11日



原文:

What's the alternative to stumpleupon toolbar? it was a great experience and it was a great tool.

For those who don't know, it was a tool to give you websites randomly and you upvote what you like and what you dislike, if you bored and want to surf the internet randomly and this also helps you bookmark websites as well, more virtual bookmark method.
